Block 01 · Upper/Lower
Training Block 01 — 4-Week Mesocycle
Upper / Lower
Strength & Size
◆

Four days a week. Each day opens with one heavy barbell strength anchor, then hypertrophy accessories for size. Chest, back, delts & arms 3×/week; legs 1×/week.

Strength anchor = heavy lead lift, 4–6 repsRIR = reps left in the tank

I can't see you lift — film your heavy anchor sets to check form. Anything sharp in a joint is a stop-and-see-a-pro signal, not a push-through.

Warm-up · ~15 min
Raise · 3 min — easy bike, row, or arm-bike until you break a light sweat.
Mobilise & activate
Arm Circles
2 × 15 each way
Band Pull-Apart
2 × 20
Band External Rotation
2 × 12 / side
Cat–Cow (T-spine)
8 slow reps
World's Greatest Stretch
5 / side
Potentiate · 5–6 min — ramp the first lift: empty bar ×10, then 2–3 sets of 3–5 reps building to your first working weight (~5 RIR; prep, not work).
The work
  1. Barbell Bench Press
    4 × 4–6 · 2–3 RIR · 3 min
    Strength Anchor
    Your main press — controlled eccentric, no bounce, add load across the block.
  2. Cable Fly
    3 × 12–15 · 1 RIR · 75s
    Chest isolation — full stretch, squeeze at the middle. Added for chest size.
  3. Chest-Supported Row
    4 × 8–10 · 2 RIR · 2 min
    Squeeze the blades, no torso swing.
  4. Seated DB Shoulder Press
    3 × 8–10 · 2 RIR · 2 min
    Stop just shy of lockout to keep tension on the delts.
  5. Lat Pulldown (wide grip)
    3 × 10–12 · 1–2 RIR · 90s
    Drive elbows to hips, minimal lean-back.
  6. Cable Lateral Raise
    4 × 12–15 · 1 RIR · 75s
    Your width-maker — lead with the elbow, zero swing.
  7. Incline DB Curl
    3 × 10–12 · 1–2 RIR · 75s
    Full stretch at the bottom, no half reps.
  8. Overhead Cable Triceps Extension
    3 × 10–12 · 1–2 RIR · 75s
    Elbows tucked, stretch the long head.
  9. Cable Crunch
    3 × 12–15 · 1 RIR · 60s
    Weighted ab builder — round the spine and add load over time. Your main abs move.
Cardio 15–30 min incline walk, easy pace.
Warm-up · ~18–20 min
Raise · 4–5 min — bike or incline walk.
Mobilise & activate
Bodyweight Squat
2 × 12
Walking Lunge
10 / leg
Glute Bridge
2 × 15
World's Greatest Stretch
5 / side
Inchworm
6 reps
Ankle Circles
10 / side
Potentiate · 6–7 min — ramp the squat: light ×8, then 2–3 sets of 3–5 reps building to your working load (~5 RIR).
The work
  1. Back Squat
    4 × 4–6 · 2 RIR · 3–4 min
    Strength Anchor
    Depth over ego load; brace hard before every rep. The strength driver for your legs.
  2. Romanian Deadlift
    3 × 8–10 · 2 RIR · 2–3 min
    Push hips back, neutral spine, feel the hamstring stretch.
  3. Leg Press
    3 × 10–15 · 1–2 RIR · 2 min
    Quad volume without loading the spine.
  4. Lying Leg Curl
    3 × 10–12 · 1–2 RIR · 90s
    Slow negative, full range of motion.
  5. Standing Calf Raise
    4 × 10–15 · 1 RIR · 60s
    1s pause and a full stretch at the bottom.
  6. Leg Extension
    2 × 12–15 · 1 RIR · 60s
    Optional finisher — only if time and energy remain.
  7. Hanging Leg Raise
    3 × 10–15 · 1–2 RIR · 60s
    Control the lower, no swinging.
Cardio Optional 10–15 min easy spin — long session, don't overcook it.
Warm-up · ~15 min
Raise · 3 min — easy bike, row, or arm-bike until you break a light sweat.
Mobilise & activate
Arm Circles
2 × 15 each way
Band Pull-Apart
2 × 20
Band External Rotation
2 × 12 / side
Cat–Cow (T-spine)
8 slow reps
World's Greatest Stretch
5 / side
Potentiate · 5–6 min — ramp the first lift: empty bar ×10, then 2–3 sets of 3–5 reps building to your first working weight (~5 RIR; prep, not work).
The work
  1. Lat Pulldown — Heavy
    4 × 5–6 · 2–3 RIR · 3 min
    Strength Anchor
    Your vertical-pull strength lift — load it heavy and add weight weekly. Same muscles as a pull-up, and it builds the exact strength your pull-up needs.
  2. Incline Barbell or DB Press (30°)
    4 × 6–8 · 2 RIR · 2–3 min
    Even tempo, deep but pain-free stretch.
  3. Pull-up Negatives or Band-Assisted Pull-up
    3 × 3–5 · 1–2 RIR · 2 min
    Jump or step to the top, lower 3–5s slow — or use a band for full reps. This is what earns your first real pull-ups.
  4. Standing DB or Machine Lateral Raise
    4 × 12–15 · 1 RIR · 75s
    More side-delt volume for the capped-shoulder look.
  5. Reverse Pec Deck
    3 × 12–15 · 1 RIR · 60s
    Rear delts for 3D shoulders and posture.
  6. Hammer Curl
    3 × 10–12 · 1–2 RIR · 75s
    Brachialis and forearm thickness.
  7. Rope Triceps Pushdown
    3 × 12–15 · 1 RIR · 60s
    Spread the rope apart at the bottom.
  8. Hanging Knee or Leg Raise
    3 × 10–15 · 1–2 RIR · 60s
    Lower-ab focus — control the lower, no swing. Added for abs.
Cardio 15–30 min incline walk, easy pace.
Warm-up · ~15 min
Raise · 3 min — easy bike, row, or arm-bike until you break a light sweat.
Mobilise & activate
Arm Circles
2 × 15 each way
Band Pull-Apart
2 × 20
Band External Rotation
2 × 12 / side
Cat–Cow (T-spine)
8 slow reps
World's Greatest Stretch
5 / side
Potentiate · 5–6 min — ramp the first lift: empty bar ×10, then 2–3 sets of 3–5 reps building to your first working weight (~5 RIR; prep, not work).
The work
  1. Barbell Overhead Press
    4 × 5–6 · 2–3 RIR · 3 min
    Strength Anchor
    Standing, brace glutes and abs, press in a straight line. Pressing power + capped delts.
  2. Flat DB Press or Machine Chest Press
    3 × 8–12 · 1–2 RIR · 2 min
    Chest, full range, controlled.
  3. Incline Dumbbell Fly
    3 × 12–15 · 1 RIR · 75s
    Upper-chest isolation — soft elbows, big stretch at the bottom, squeeze up. Easy setup: ~30° bench + two dumbbells.
  4. Single-Arm DB Row
    3 × 10–12 · 2 RIR · 90s
    Hand/knee on the bench, row the DB to your hip; back thickness.
  5. Lateral Raise (cable or DB)
    4 × 12–20 · 1 RIR · 60s
    High-rep width — chase the burn.
  6. Cable Face Pull
    3 × 15–20 · 1 RIR · 60s
    Rope to your forehead, elbows high; rear delts + shoulder health.
  7. EZ-Bar Curl
    3 × 10–12 · 1 RIR · 75s
    Angled bar — easier on the wrists than a straight bar.
  8. Triceps Dips or Pushdown
    3 × 10–15 · 1 RIR · 75s
    Lean slightly forward on parallel bars, or use the assisted-dip machine.
Cardio 15–30 min incline walk, easy pace.

Strength anchors — bench, squat, pulldown, OHP

Stay in the 4–6 range. Hit 6 reps on all 4 sets at the target RIR → add load next week (~2.5 kg upper / 5 kg lower, or the smallest jump available) → reset to the bottom of the range. Small, consistent load increases are the whole game.

Hypertrophy accessories

Double progression: chase the top of each range at a fixed load (RIR drops naturally), then add load and reset. Week 2: add 1 set to one lateral-raise slot and one row.

Week 3

Push the anchors toward 1–2 RIR; take isolations to 1 RIR.

Anchors: 2 sets at ~60–65% of your week-3 load — fast and crisp (~5 RIR), focus on bar speed and technique.

Accessories: 2 sets at ~60% load @ 4–5 RIR.

If your week-3 numbers show you're recovering well, message me and we bridge straight into Block 2 instead.

After lifting: 15–30 min low-intensity work — pick one:

Incline walk
15–30 min
Easy bike
low intensity

At this intensity it won't dent your strength or size, and lifting-then-cardio is the right order.

The real fat-loss levers: ~8,000–10,000 steps a day and your diet — those move the needle far more than the finisher, at zero cost to recovery.

Run week 1, then send me your anchor loads & reps (bench, squat, pulldown, OHP) plus accessory top sets — I'll set week 2 off your real numbers.
